context_summary: M1 ship-gate closed (Phase 7, 2026-07-12). M2 in flight: Phase 8 entity resolution (closed 2026-07-13), Phase 9 RAG recall (closed 2026-07-21), Phase 10 Wiki-LLM Waves 1-4 (closed 2026-07-21; 10-01-SUMMARY.md on disk), Phase 11 SOCMINT + Arctic Waves 1-6 (closed 2026-07-22 per 11-01-SUMMARY.md). Phase 12 (CNR alerting / dissemination) is next and ready to plan. Forensic housekeeping audit (STATE/HANDOFF/PROJECT/ROADMAP-999.4/REQUIREMENTS-paths/999.{3,4}-archive) committed today as a single chore.

phase_11_closeout.summary: Phase 11 (SOCMINT + Arctic) closed 2026-07-22. Waves 1-6 complete: ADR-014 SOCMINT legal/ToS, discipline + Admiralty reliability provenance schema, MCP adapters for Telegram (Telethon) + BarentsWatch (AIS), on-demand translation (Phase 999.1 closed via Wave 4), YouTube local faster-whisper transcript (Wave 5). ACLED gate keeps unlicensed data out. Full pytest suite 518 passed / 61 skipped (default); integration suite 579/0/0.

999.2.rationale: Dedup threshold calibration on larger corpus — same-day R2 spike was too narrow. Open into the first M2 phase that runs controlled off-topic-vs-same-event evaluation.

999.3.status: CLOSED 2026-07-12 (mE5-large threshold re-validated at T*=0.92; live --corpus-from-postgres GO at T*=0.92)

999.4.status: CLOSED 2026-07-22 (shipped as Phase 10 Wave 4 — verify_language_coverage() in libs/contracts/_verify.py)

human_actions_pending: Operator decision: begin Phase 12 planning (CNR alerting / dissemination). The next GSD dispatch is /gsd-discuss-phase 12 to surface scope and constraints, then /gsd-plan-phase 12 to lock success criteria and waves.
Operator decision: push the housekeeping chore commit to origin/main when ready (project rule keeps push as an explicit operator action).

decisions.rationale: Real mE5-large vector re-validation (backlog 999.3) recommended 0.92 via conservative overmerge==0 fallback; 0.85 was the old R3 bge-m3-misparse value and is no longer used.
Entity Graph.md projection should show actual aliases (mention + lang) rather than a bare count; alias_count removed as redundant with len(aliases).

decisions.decision: Row-based and store-backed Entity Graph both render type and language
decisions.rationale: Consistency between the two graph formats; type/lang are canonical entity attributes.

decisions.decision: Entity NER in triage worker guarded by asyncio.wait_for timeout
decisions.rationale: Prevents a hung LLM NER call from blocking verdict.ready publication per ADR-004 R5 prohibition.

decisions.decision: Cyrillic↔Latin transliteration added to scripts/validate_entity_threshold.py for cross-language corpus validation
decisions.rationale: Enables --corpus-from-postgres to pair Cyrillic and Latin mentions of the same entity (e.g. NATO / НАТО); live run validated the cross-language bar at T*=0.92 (GO).

decisions_recorded_this_audit.rationale: 6 forensic findings from /gsd-progress --forensic + reviewer follow-ups (DI-2 [OUT], 999.4 STATE mention, lost Phase 8 decisions restored, 999.3/999.4 directories archived, Phase 10 10-01-SUMMARY.md tracked) all addressed in a single chore commit. Phase 12 now plans from clean paper.

context_notes: All integer phases 0-11 are closed. Phase 99.1, 999.3, 999.4 closed and archived. Phase 999.2 still BACKLOG with rationale carried forward; the carry-forward rationale is still valid (no movement signal since R2 spike) and unrelated to Phase 12. phase_13_ingest_body_wiring.note: apps/ingest-{gmail,imap,youtube,telegram,barentswatch,acled,obsidian} currently UPSERT only body_ref. To populate the new body TEXT column, each ingest service needs a parallel write in PostgresStore.put_item. Until then production articles.body stays NULL and the link view reads summary-only.

phase_12_phase_13_depend.verified_by: post-ADR-015 ordering-validation pass (gsd-progress --next dispatch; phase_12_phase_13_depend ask)

sql_evidence: apps/brief/main.py::_ENRICHMENT_SQL fetches a.body (extended in feat(schema) commit 3f36dcf)
apps/brief/consumer.py::_SELECT fetches e.item_id, e.ccir, e.cnr, e.score, e.bucket, e.why, e.pmesii, e.tessoc, a.title, a.summary, a.body, a.source, a.url, a.ts
apps/brief/renderer.py::render_links haystack = ' '.join(title, summary, body) -> URL extraction via _OUTBOUND_URL_RE
apps/triage/triage_score.py scores over TITLE + SUMMARY fields only; never reads body as a scoring input (per Phase 999.2 R2 spec: `title + summary[:512]` only)

phase_12_phase_13_depend.do_not_flip_ordering_rationale: Phase 12 alerting has no structural dependency on Phase 13. The alert payload (per ADR-015 Decision 3) derives from enrichment row fields + summary only; the deep_link targets Obsidian vault, NOT the link view. Body is neither read nor propagated into the push payload. Phase 12 ships in summary-only mode without any atomic rollback risk.
phase_12_phase_13_depend.soft_dependency_detail: The link view (apps/brief/views.py?view=links) goes through render_links() which reads body. Until Phase 13 wires body population by apps/ingest-*, all body fields are NULL on production rows, so the link view's URL extraction is summary-only and under-extracts outbound URLs (the entire reason feat(schema) added the articles.body TEXT column in the first place).
phase_12_phase_13_depend.operational_impact: CAT I hits are rare (~1-5/day typical); each CAT I alert independently fires and the operator's primary action after a CAT I alert is the Obsidian deep-link (containing the article body in full once Phase 13 lands, currently the article title + summary). The link view is a downstream reading-queue surface the operator visits AFTER reading the alert item, so degraded link-view URL coverage is recovered (not lost) when the operator reads via Obsidian directly.
phase_12_phase_13_depend.do_flip_ordering_if: The operator flags the link view as a CRITICAL CAT I downstream surface (i.e. the operator's CAT I workflow is `ntfy -> link view` rather than `ntfy -> Obsidian note`). If so, ADR-016 to flip ordering lands at discuss-phase Turn-2 and Phase 13 ships before Phase 12.
phase_12_phase_13_depend.discuss_phase_ask_turn_1: Surface the link-view-degraded-UX trade-off to the operator explicitly. Acceptable default: parallel with link view summary-only during Phase 12 stage-1 deployments, Phase 13 wiring lifts body population for full URL extraction later. Operator confirms the CW I workflow (`ntfy -> Obsidian` vs. `ntfy -> link view`) to drive the flip decision.

adr_004_resolved.historical_note: ADR-004 was never committed to docs/adr/ (git ls-files, deletion log, fsck, reflog, log --grep ADR-004 all return zero hits); the operative stance was cross-referenced as 'ADR-004' across ROADMAP/STATE/ARCHITECTURE/ADR-014/docker-compose/llm-router; ADR-016 codifies it.
